evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Lead the resolution of critical data issues, establishing monitoring systems and best practices to prevent future problems and improve data reliability.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities emphasize data reliability, monitoring, semantic layer governance, and long-term sustainability with no velocity/speed language present at all.
evidence.domain_risk: developing data capabilities that power decisions across our global organization
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Audiobook/e-book streaming analytics informs business decisions but carries no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance exposure.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Our team is composed of Analytics Engineers and Data Engineers, fostering a collaborative environment that enables us to manage data end to end
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named stack in active use (BigQuery, Looker, dbt, GCP) and an existing semantic layer show the function exists and is growing, with room to "evaluate and introduce new technologies as we scale."
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop data transformation steps and build a strong data modeling foundation for enabling self-service analytics across the company.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Responsibilities name concrete deliverables (semantic layer, data transformation steps, monitoring systems) precise enough to picture the day-to-day work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Build and maintain trusting, working relationships with business partners across domains and discover high-impact opportunities to enable self-service analytics in our semantic layer.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The emphasis is on serving internal data consumers via self-service analytics and a semantic layer, not a specific commercial, product, or finance function.
evidence.autonomy_level: Strategically plan and deliver meaningful projects to increase data literacy at Storytel.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs like "strategically plan", "lead the resolution", and "drive adoption" place this role in a direction-setting, ownership-oriented posture.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The JD contains no mention of AI tools, AI-assisted coding, or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in responsibilities or requirements.
evidence.testing_framing: Lead the resolution of critical data issues, establishing monitoring systems and best practices to prevent future problems and improve data reliability.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality/reliability is framed as an owned responsibility with an accountable verb ("lead the resolution", "establishing monitoring systems"), not a tool in a skills list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Lead the resolution of critical data issues, establishing monitoring systems and best practices to prevent future problems and improve data reliability.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability and prevention of future data issues are a secondary but clear concern, without regulatory or compliance framing, matching moderate operational-reliability risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: with opportunities to evaluate and introduce new technologies as we scale
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role builds new modeling foundations and semantic layer opportunities while also fixing critical data issues and scaling existing tooling, a mix of both.
evidence.language_gate: please submit your application in English
evidence.loss_aversion: establishing monitoring systems and best practices to prevent future problems and improve data reliability — the fear is data reliability failures, not compliance or regulatory exposure.
